title: Gameplay Framework
description: Understand GameMode, PlayerController, Pawn, Character, GameState, and PlayerState in Unreal.
learningObjectives: Identify responsibilities of the Unreal Gameplay Framework classes
Configure custom GameMode rules and assign default spawning pawn classes
Explain the division between PlayerController input logic and Pawn physical simulation representation

sections.title: Gameplay Architecture Classes
sections.content:
Unreal divides game rules, simulation representations, and player inputs into dedicated classes:

1. **GameModeBase**: Rules of the game (e.g. victory conditions, default spawns). Only exists on the server.
2. **PlayerController**: The interface translating player physical inputs into gameplay actions.
3. **Pawn**: Any actor that can be possessed by a controller and receive inputs.
4. **Character**: A specialized Pawn containing a Movement Component designed for walking, jumping, and swimming simulation.

sections.title: Possession Flow
sections.content: When a level loads, the GameMode instantiates the PlayerController. The controller then possess the player character Pawn, establishing the link to route hardware keyboard/mouse signals to movement vectors.
